title: thymus villosus subsp. villosus
intro: Thymus villosus subsp. villosus, also known as the thyme thyme, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is a herbaceous perennial with a woody base and purple flowers. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and aid in digestion. Thymus villosus subsp. villosus can be prepared as an herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used to boost the immune system, relieve respiratory issues, and promote overall health and well-being.
